findings:
  - { id: judged-df-1, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-1 keeps a 'grandfathered v0 boards' section as a surviving home affordance, but feature dc-2 declares the directory groups by status — drafts in progress, accepted-pending-build, active components, terminal — with 'the status is the distinction, never the address.' A grandfathered-v0-boards section is a grouping keyed on provenance/vintage, not on any of the four declared status groups, and no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against feature dc-2 for this carve-out. (confidence 0.45)", disposition: no-conflict, note: "feature dc-2 governs how SPEC entries are grouped in the directory; the grandfathered v0 boards (data/mutable/boards/*.json, R4-I-9: superseded but history never rewritten) are not spec entries and join no status group — they remain a separate legacy affordance beneath the directory, exactly as the disclosures pointer and services sections do", target_ref: spec/workbench-directory }
  - { id: judged-df-2, kind: judged, text: "Feature dc-5 requires 'each entry disclosed by source' (local design ref vs remote-tracking ref) in the index/directory, but story dc-3 declares the local/remote split is enforced 'by the routing story behind the one grammar, never by the directory minting different link shapes' — deferring all local-vs-remote disclosure to the routed board page. If the directory renders identical, undifferentiated entries for local and remote-only branches, the per-entry source disclosure feature dc-5 demands is dropped at the directory surface, and no exempts edge against feature dc-5 declares that narrowing. (confidence 0.35)", disposition: no-conflict, note: "the directory does NOT render local and remote entries undifferentiated: story ac-1/ac-2 mandate per-entry source disclosure on the directory page itself ('every directory entry is disclosed by source — local design branch vs remote-tracking, feature dc-5'); dc-3 unifies only the LINK SHAPE, and the source disclosure is an on-entry chip, not a link-shape difference", target_ref: spec/workbench-directory }

A grandfathered-v0-boards section is a grouping keyed on provenance/vintage, not on any of the four declared status groups, and no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against feature dc-2 for this carve-out.\",\"confidence\":0.45,\"target\":\"spec/workbench-directory\"},{\"id\":\"df-2\",\"text\":\"Feature dc-5 requires 'each entry disclosed by source' (local design ref vs remote-tracking ref) in the index/directory, but story dc-3 declares the local/remote split is enforced 'by the routing story behind the one grammar, never by the directory minting different link shapes' — deferring all local-vs-remote disclosure to the routed board page. If the directory renders identical, undifferentiated entries for local and remote-only branches, the per-entry source disclosure feature dc-5 demands is dropped at the directory surface, and no exempts edge against feature dc-5 declares that narrowing.\",\"confidence\":0.35,\"target\":\"spec/workbench-directory\"}]}" }

---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-df-1** [no-conflict]: Story dc-1 keeps a 'grandfathered v0 boards' section as a surviving home affordance, but feature dc-2 declares the directory groups by status — drafts in progress, accepted-pending-build, active components, terminal — with 'the status is the distinction, never the address.' A grandfathered-v0-boards section is a grouping keyed on provenance/vintage, not on any of the four declared status groups, and no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against feature dc-2 for this carve-out. (confidence 0.45) — feature dc-2 governs how SPEC entries are grouped in the directory; the grandfathered v0 boards (data/mutable/boards/*.json, R4-I-9: superseded but history never rewritten) are not spec entries and join no status group — they remain a separate legacy affordance beneath the directory, exactly as the disclosures pointer and services sections do
- **judged-df-2** [no-conflict]: Feature dc-5 requires 'each entry disclosed by source' (local design ref vs remote-tracking ref) in the index/directory, but story dc-3 declares the local/remote split is enforced 'by the routing story behind the one grammar, never by the directory minting different link shapes' — deferring all local-vs-remote disclosure to the routed board page. If the directory renders identical, undifferentiated entries for local and remote-only branches, the per-entry source disclosure feature dc-5 demands is dropped at the directory surface, and no exempts edge against feature dc-5 declares that narrowing. (confidence 0.35) — the directory does NOT render local and remote entries undifferentiated: story ac-1/ac-2 mandate per-entry source disclosure on the directory page itself ('every directory entry is disclosed by source — local design branch vs remote-tracking, feature dc-5'); dc-3 unifies only the LINK SHAPE, and the source disclosure is an on-entry chip, not a link-shape difference
